title: Parliamentary Committee Secretariat
companySize: Small committee office supporting a committee of MPs or senators
description: Committee secretariat staff run the engine room of parliamentary scrutiny. They organise hearings, brief members, handle submissions, draft reports, and keep evidence flowing cleanly into parliamentary work.

schedule.activity: Review witness list, hearing logistics, and overnight correspondence
Prepare briefing notes for committee members
Run a hearing: witness management, timekeeping, evidence handling
Lunch while cleaning up follow-up actions from the morning session
Analyse written submissions and cluster themes for members
Draft sections of the committee report
Clear next-step notes and witness follow-up correspondence

challenges: Heavy document handling under time pressure
Staying neutral while supporting political members
Managing sensitive submissions and reputational risk
rewards: Deep exposure to scrutiny and evidence processes
Strong writing and institutional literacy development
Front-row view of how parliament tests claims
careerPath: Move into Parliament, APS, inquiry work, or oversight roles
